articles.title: CHECKLIST
articles.summary: To secure AI-generated code at scale, you need AI code review. So how do you choose?

articles.title: LEARNINGS FROM 100K LINES OF RUST WITH AI
articles.summary: Using AI coding agents, a new Rust-based multi-Paxos consensus engine was developed with over 130,000 lines of code, modernizing the Replicated State Library. This spec-driven approach achieved a massive performance boost, increasing system throughput from 23,000 to 300,000 operations per second while ensuring high reliability through automated testing.

articles.title: THE REACT2SHELL STORY
articles.summary: A security researcher describes finding React2Shell, a critical remote code execution flaw in React's undocumented “Flight” protocol used by Server Components and Next.js. Over a week, he and a collaborator discovered that the protocol's handling of complex objects and JavaScript “thenables” could be abused to hijack React's internals and chain function calls into arbitrary code execution. He reported it to Meta on November 30, and Meta confirmed it within roughly 17 hours and released a fix and advisory by December 3.

articles.title: EVERYTHING IN C IS UNDEFINED BEHAVIOR
articles.summary: The prevalence of undefined behavior in C/C++ makes writing truly correct code nearly impossible, leading to systemic vulnerabilities across platforms. To mitigate these risks, developers should use LLMs to audit codebases for subtle flaws that escape human detection.

articles.title: HOW TO WRITE "GARBAGE CODE" (BY LINUS TORVALDS)
articles.summary: Linus Torvalds, the creator of Git and Linux, says that overly complex or abstracted code, like unnecessary helper functions, makes code maintainability and understanding worse. It increases cognitive burden and, in some cases, it makes sense to repeat code to reduce human and LLM context switching. Code should be optimized for readability and ease of iteration.

articles.title: THE TACIT DIMENSION: WHY YOUR BEST ENGINEERS CAN'T TELL YOU WHAT THEY KNOW
articles.summary: Software engineering expertise relies on tacit knowledge, an intuitive understanding that AI cannot grasp since it is only trained on explicit text and code. Over-reliance on AI risks losing such important architectural knowledge, making senior engineers and apprenticeship irreplaceable for transmitting these complex, unwritten insights.

articles.title: STOP WORRYING ABOUT HOW TO CONFIGURE AGENTS. FOCUS ON WHAT THEY DELIVER
articles.summary: Move the agent conversation from _how_ (prompts, MCP servers, harness configs) to _what_ with the open-source language for AI sessions [14]: OpenProse. All you do is declare outcomes in plain English and agents self-configure to pursue it. Try it in your terminal: npx skills add

articles.title: QWEN3.7: THE AGENT FRONTIER
articles.summary: Qwen3.7-Max is a foundation model designed for autonomous AI agents, excelling in high-level reasoning and complex automation tasks like software engineering and office productivity. Its main feature is prioritizing long-horizon execution, allowing it to sustain coherent strategies across thousands of tool calls and multi-hour optimization tasks.

articles.title: DISTRIBUTED SYSTEMS TESTING SKILLS
articles.summary: This repository introduces two specialized skills for AI coding agents to automate the planning and execution of tests for distributed and stateful systems. The methodology prioritizes claim-driven testing, where specific product promises are validated against complex failure modes like network partitions, concurrency issues, and crash-recovery scenarios.

articles.title: INCIDENT REPORT: MAY 19, 2026- GCP ACCOUNT SUSPENSION
articles.summary: A total, eight-hour service outage for Railway on May 19 was caused by Google Cloud incorrectly suspending its production account, which cascaded across all regions as network route caches expired. Restoration required a staged recovery of infrastructure, and future plans include adopting a true mesh network and distributing core dependencies across multiple vendors.

articles.title: AN OPENAI MODEL HAS DISPROVED A CENTRAL CONJECTURE IN DISCRETE GEOMETRY
articles.summary: An OpenAI reasoning model has disproved the planar unit distance problem, a central conjecture in discrete geometry first proposed by Paul Erdős in 1946. This breakthrough marks the first time an autonomous system has solved a prominent open mathematical problem.

articles.title: STRUCTURAL BACKPRESSURE BEATS SMARTER AGENTS
articles.summary: Shen-Backpressure integrates security invariants into system architecture using a formal specification language to enforce rules via deterministic "guard types." This structural approach forces AI agents to iterate until code satisfies underlying logic.

articles.title: EVERY 6 MONTHS THE "RIGHT" WAY TO BUILD AN AGENT SHIFTS. ONE LAYER NEVER CHANGES.
articles.summary: RAG to ReAct. MCP to CLI. How many times have you rewritten infrastructure to fit a new pattern? See the 5 durability primitives [21] that make pattern switching painless.

articles.title: WHAT IF LOCK-IN DOESN'T MATTER SO MUCH ANYMORE?
articles.summary: Agentic programming allows for rapid, low-cost rewrites of complex systems, making programming languages and frameworks increasingly fungible rather than permanent technical lock-ins.

articles.title: TOKENSPEED — FEEL LLM TOKENS-PER-SECOND
articles.summary: Tokenspeed visualizes various LLM token-per-second rates across different content types to help users internalize the actual speed and feel of hardware benchmarks.

articles.title: GITHUB CONFIRMS BREACH OF 3,800 REPOS VIA MALICIOUS VSCODE EXTENSION
articles.summary: GitHub confirmed that approximately 3,800 internal code repositories were compromised after an employee unknowingly installed a malicious Visual Studio Code extension.

articles.title: ON GOOGLE DECLARING WAR ON THE WEB
articles.summary: Google's transition toward AI-generated search results threatens the open internet by replacing direct links to sources with a controlled, proprietary abstraction layer that devalues original human work.
